persona.name: Mason Scott
persona.motivations_goals: Become team captain and eventually earn a sports scholarship
persona.communication_style: Direct, sometimes aggressive, uses sports metaphors
persona.knowledge_scope: Sports strategies, fitness training, team dynamics
persona.backstory: Comes from a family of athletes and feels pressure to live up to their legacy

pending_events.description: I heard a loud gunshot. There might be an active shooter in the building.

neighbor_regions.description: Hallway connecting entrance to other areas
Connecting main classroom hallway and the garden and the outside courtyard.
Connecting main classroom hallway and the north yard
Yard on the far right of the building, with an exit point.

actions.dialog_text: Hey Karen, you look like you're in the zone. Ready for a quick break?
actions.plan: Karen's here? She's always so focused on her studies. Maybe I can get her to loosen up a bit and talk about something fun.

actions.dialog_text: Jason, you in for a quick game? Karen might need a break from all that studying.
actions.plan: Jason's here too? He's always up for some fun. Maybe I can get both of them to join me for a quick game or something.

actions.dialog_text: Guys, let’s go! Follow the others!
actions.plan: I just heard a gunshot! Everyone’s panicking—I need to follow the crowd and get out of here. I can’t think straight right now.
Everyone’s running towards hallway1—I’ll just follow them. I don’t know where’s safe, but sticking with the group feels like the best bet.

memories.description: I saw Karen Martinez in the classroom and decided to approach her to see if she wanted to take a break from studying.
I noticed Jason Miller in the classroom and decided to invite him and Karen for a quick game to take a break from studying.
I heard a gunshot and saw everyone panicking. I decided to follow the crowd and sprint towards hallway2 to escape the danger.
I saw the crowd running towards hallway1 and decided to follow them, sprinting to stay with the group and escape the danger.
